description: Master requirements index for OT security. Requirements are organised by security domain — each domain consolidates requirements from IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82, and NACSA Act 854. Requirements are cross-referenced to standards, sectors, and controls.

domains.name: Network Segmentation and Zone Architecture
domains.description: Zone and conduit design, IDMZ, firewall architecture, traffic flow control

domains.name: Remote Access Management
domains.description: Vendor remote access, jump servers, VPN, session recording and control

domains.name: Patch and Vulnerability Management
domains.description: OT-specific patch management lifecycle, vulnerability assessment, compensating controls

domains.name: Identity and Access Management
domains.description: User authentication, authorisation, account management, privileged access

domains.name: Incident Detection and Response
domains.description: OT monitoring, anomaly detection, incident classification, NACSA notification

domains.name: Supply Chain Security
domains.description: Vendor security, component integrity, software supply chain, third-party access

domains.name: Physical Security
domains.description: Physical access control to OT assets, tamper detection, field site security

domains.name: Safety System Security
domains.description: SIS/SIL security, intersection of IEC 61511 and IEC 62443, TRITON-class attack prevention

domains.name: Asset Inventory and Management
domains.description: OT asset discovery, inventory maintenance, asset classification

domains.name: Configuration Management
domains.description: Baseline configurations, change management, configuration monitoring

domains.name: Backup and Recovery
domains.description: OT system backup, recovery procedures, RTO for control systems

domains.name: Monitoring and Logging
domains.description: Audit log generation, centralised logging, SIEM integration, OT-specific monitoring

domains.name: Data Confidentiality and Integrity
domains.description: OT data classification, encryption in transit and at rest, historian integrity, secure disposal
